content: We can frame the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a base architecture to instantiate structured multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by modifying Self-Refine’s single self-feedback loop to incorporate inconsistent alternative solving perspectives from existing reflection research. This creates a hybrid approach that retains Self-Refine’s advantage of no additional training or labeled data while solving the common limitation of single-perspective blind spots in naive self-feedback. This hybrid framework can systematically improve generation quality for code, reasoning, and open-ended text generation without extra infrastructure overhead.
